channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Multi-storey home destroyed in Yarralumla fire
item.description: A major fire has completely destroyed a multi-storey home in Yarralumla, located in Canberra's inner south. Emergency services were called to the scene on Schomburgk Street around 7:29pm on Friday, Ju...

item.title: Political Tensions Escalate Over Land Dispute Involving Leader's Family
item.description: The article details a growing political controversy surrounding a high-profile leader's family entangled in a land dispute. The conflict has sparked widespread public protests, with allegations of cor...

item.title: Tanzania's Royal Clinic Achieves 28,000 Patient Milestone in First Year
item.description: The Royal, International Patients and Master Health Check-up Clinic at Benjamin Mkapa Hospital in Dodoma has treated over 28,000 patients in its first year, including more than 200 international clien...

item.title: Timeline of IU and Lee Jong-suk's Relationship from Colleagues to Lovers and Breakup
item.description: The article details the evolving relationship between South Korean celebrities IU (Lee Ji-eun) and Lee Jong-suk, tracing their journey from distant colleagues to lovers and eventual breakup. Their pat...

item.title: Jetboat Collision at Queenstown Tourist Spot Leaves Five with Minor Injuries
item.description: A Shotover Jet boat with 14 people on board crashed into rocks at a tourist hotspot in Queenstown during the busy school holiday period, leaving five individuals with minor injuries. Emergency service...

item.title: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari Warns Against Communal Tensions After Reckless Remarks by MLA Humayun Kabir
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari criticized MLA Humayun Kabir for making inflammatory remarks during a rally in Rejinagar, which is set to host a bypoll. Adhikari accused Kabir of attemptin...

item.title: Jennie performs Tame Impala remix 'Dracula' at Mad Cool 2026
item.description: JENNIE, member of BLACKPINK, delivered a high-energy performance at Mad Cool 2026, showcasing her remix of Tame Impala’s track 'Dracula'. The 17-track set included unreleased songs like 'Heaven' and '...

item.title: Indiana's Childcare Crisis and Regulatory Challenges
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing childcare crisis in Indiana, highlighting a recent case of an unlicensed childcare program in Goshen that operated with over 85 children and only three adults. This i...

item.title: Weather Alert: Batanes Remains Under Signal No. 2 as Typhoon 'Inday' Approaches PAR Exit
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) maintains Signal No. 2 for typhoon warning in Batanes as Typhoon 'Inday' approaches the PAR (Philippine Area o...

item.title: Former Homeland Security Official Warns of Midterm Election Manipulation Plot Involving Trump's 'Boyhood Pal'
item.description: Former Homeland Security official Miles Taylor has warned of an alleged plot to manipulate the midterm elections, with President Donald Trump's longtime friend Peter Ticktin at its center. Ticktin, an...

item.title: ACT Government Purchases Ginninderra East CSIRO Land for $385 Million to Deliver More Than 3,000 Homes
item.description: The ACT Government has finalised the purchase of 243 hectares of the former CSIRO Ginninderra research station in Canberra’s north for $385 million, ending more than a decade of discussions about the ...

item.title: Calgary Group Wins $100K in Lotto Max Lottery
item.description: A local group from the Calgary area has claimed a $100,000 prize from the Lotto Max lottery, marking a significant win for the community. The article highlights the group's unexpected success, emphasi...

item.title: Australia's Foreign Policy Balances Security and Trade in Pacific Tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Foreign Minister Penny Wong are intensifying diplomatic efforts in the Pacific region to balance national security concerns with trade interests. The week of engage...

item.title: Kenya's IWPG and NCIC Collaborate to Empower Women in Peacebuilding and Community Cohesion
item.description: The International Women's Peace Group (IWPG) has partnered with the National Cohesion and Integration Commission (NCIC) to strengthen women-led peacebuilding efforts and community cohesion across Keny...

item.title: Telus CEO Victor Dodig Faces Challenges with Dividend Policy and Market Uncertainty
item.description: The article discusses the potential changes in Telus' dividend policy under new CEO Victor Dodig, who took over from Darren Entwistle. Telus shares have declined by 57% over four years due to slowing ...

item.title: Senegal's Constitutional Council Rejects Ousmane Sonko's Bill
item.description: The constitutional council of Senegal has ruled that the bill passed by PASTEF lawmakers on June 29 is unconstitutional, dealing a significant blow to Ousmane Sonko, who leads both the party and the N...

item.title: PM Shehbaz and Iran's president discuss preserving regional peace amid tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif emphasized the importance of maintaining peace achievements during a telephone conversation with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. The call comes amid heightened tensi...

item.title: South Africa FA confirms Hugo Broos remains Bafana Bafana head coach after World Cup exit
item.description: The South African Football Association (SAFA) has officially dismissed rumors that Hugo Broos has resigned as head coach of the national team after their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign. Despite reports ...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, Iconic Welsh Singer Known for Total Eclipse of the Heart, Passes Away at 75
item.description: Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler, renowned for her powerful husky voice and 1980s hits, has sadly passed away at the age of 75 in a hospital in Portugal. Tributes have poured in from across the music world a...

item.title: Tshwane Council Suspends City Manager and Investigates CFO Over Misconduct Allegations
item.description: The City of Tshwane has suspended its city manager, Johann Mettler, and initiated disciplinary proceedings against CFO Gareth Mnisi following a forensic investigation. Council executive mayor Dr Nasip...
